words.meaning: to do (impf) / to do (pf) — imperfective for ongoing/repeated, perfective for completed action
words.example: Я делаю домашнее задание каждый день. Вчера я сделал всё за час.
words.exampleMeaning: I do homework every day. Yesterday I did everything in an hour.

words.meaning: to read (impf) / to read (pf) — imperfective for process, perfective for finishing
words.example: Она читала книгу весь вечер. Она прочитала книгу за неделю.
words.exampleMeaning: She was reading a book all evening. She finished reading the book in a week.

words.meaning: to write (impf) / to write (pf) — imperfective for process, perfective for completing
words.example: Он писал письмо два часа. Он написал письмо и отправил его.
words.exampleMeaning: He was writing a letter for two hours. He wrote the letter and sent it.

words.meaning: to buy (impf) / to buy (pf) — imperfective for habitual buying, perfective for a specific purchase
words.example: Мы покупаем хлеб каждое утро. Мы купили новую машину.
words.exampleMeaning: We buy bread every morning. We bought a new car.

words.meaning: to speak/say (impf) / to say (pf) — imperfective for ongoing speech, perfective for a single statement
words.example: Он говорил долго. Он сказал мне правду.
words.exampleMeaning: He spoke for a long time. He told me the truth.

words.meaning: to open (impf) / to open (pf) — imperfective for repeated/habitual, perfective for one-time action
words.example: Магазин открывают каждый день в девять. Она открыла окно.
words.exampleMeaning: The store opens every day at nine. She opened the window.

words.meaning: to close (impf) / to close (pf) — imperfective for process or habit, perfective for completion
words.example: Не закрывайте дверь, пожалуйста. Он закрыл книгу и встал.
words.exampleMeaning: Don't close the door, please. He closed the book and stood up.

words.meaning: to give (impf) / to give (pf) — imperfective for repeated giving, perfective for a single act
words.example: Мама давала мне деньги каждую неделю. Дай мне, пожалуйста, воды.
words.exampleMeaning: Mom used to give me money every week. Give me some water, please.

words.meaning: to take (impf) / to take (pf) — imperfective for habitual, perfective for a completed action
words.example: Я всегда беру зонт с собой. Возьми эту книгу.
words.exampleMeaning: I always take an umbrella with me. Take this book.

words.meaning: to decide/solve (impf) / to decide/solve (pf) — imperfective for process, perfective for result
words.example: Мы долго решали эту задачу. Мы решили поехать в отпуск.
words.exampleMeaning: We were solving this problem for a long time. We decided to go on vacation.

words.meaning: to begin (impf) / to begin (pf) — imperfective for repeated starts, perfective for a specific start
words.example: Занятия начинаются в восемь. Он начал новый проект.
words.exampleMeaning: Classes begin at eight. He started a new project.

words.meaning: to understand (impf) / to understand (pf) — imperfective for ongoing state, perfective for sudden realization
words.example: Я не понимаю этот текст. Наконец я понял, что он имел в виду.
words.exampleMeaning: I don't understand this text. I finally understood what he meant.

words.meaning: to learn/study (impf) / to learn (pf) — imperfective for process, perfective for mastering
words.example: Я учу русский язык три года. Она выучила все слова к экзамену.
words.exampleMeaning: I have been studying Russian for three years. She learned all the words for the exam.

words.meaning: to prepare/cook (impf) / to prepare/cook (pf) — imperfective for process, perfective for result
words.example: Бабушка готовила обед два часа. Она приготовила вкусный борщ.
words.exampleMeaning: Grandma was cooking lunch for two hours. She made a delicious borscht.

words.meaning: to receive (impf) / to receive (pf) — imperfective for habitual, perfective for a specific receipt
words.example: Я получаю зарплату каждый месяц. Она получила письмо от друга.
words.exampleMeaning: I receive my salary every month. She received a letter from a friend.

words.meaning: to change (impf) / to change (pf) — imperfective for ongoing/repeated, perfective for completed change
words.example: Он часто меняет работу. Я поменял пароль.
words.exampleMeaning: He often changes jobs. I changed the password.

items.sentence: Я ___ русский язык каждый день. (study, imperfective)
Мы ___ эту задачу и нашли ответ. (solved, perfective)

items.question: What aspect would you use for 'I read every day'?
